repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
Because it looks like IPython (which is a test dependency) can contaminate the results, so run that specific test in a fresh process.

Fixes #7075

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -434,3 +434,59 @@ def py_parse_code(code):
             return result
     except Errors.CompileError as e:
         raise SyntaxError(e.message_only)
+
+
+# Utilities to run unitest classes in an isolated process.
+def _run_isolated_tests(suite, result_queue):
+    runner = unittest.TextTestRunner(resultclass=unittest.TestResult)
+    result = runner.run(suite)
+    # Clean up various redirected stdout streams to let us pickle it
+    for k in list(result.__dict__.keys()):
+        if k.startswith("_") and not k.startswith("__"):
+            delattr(result, k)
+    result_queue.put(result)
+

comment_data.body: Is this selective enough, or is there a more specific name part that would let us select the right attributes?

comment_data.body:
I think it is selective enough: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/7232883adfc28f94a62d2e79c897db59711702d7/Lib/unittest/result.py#L38. I think I should rephrase it though (although it's largely the same test).

All the public attributes that are important to copy start with a letter. But I should express it like that instead

+    result = runner.run(suite)

commit.message:
Isolate "builtins" test

Because it looks like IPython (which is a test dependency) can
contaminate the results, so run that specific test in a fresh
process.
Isolate both tests and add a shared mechanism
